methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Evolv Technology operates in Defense & Security as a public company (NASDAQ: EVLV) that has raised over $400M to date. The broader sector contains 21 peers, though the comparable universe applicable to Evolv's specific stage and valuation tier is constrained: 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method cannot be reliably applied here. Filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier comps yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category, falling below the minimum threshold for robust triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security business models, technical maturity, and go-to-market strategies across 21 companies. The identified public tickers represent different subsectors and geographies; the private set spans infrastructure, autonomy, and sensor fusion—each with distinct unit economics and deployment timelines.

Without sufficient narrow-peer density, a credible valuation range cannot be supported by comparable-company analysis alone. Alternative methods—discounted cash flow analysis informed by sector growth rates, precedent transactions, or asset-based approaches—would be required to triangulate a defensible valuation. The key uncertainty is Evolv's position within the Defense & Security landscape relative to both the public comps and the high-valuation private cohort, which cannot be resolved through peer multiples.
